label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so the user is asking about the main topic of the passage provided. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand it. The passage starts by talking about how both men and women are surrounded by diet, exercise, and fashion tips in media. It mentions that men no longer just need a nice suit to be considered smart and handsome. Then it goes on to cite a survey from the University of the West of England saying that 80% of men are unhappy with their bodies, specifically mentioning beer bellies and lack of muscles. The text then discusses the influence of role models in media leading people to have a perception of the "perfect" body. There's a mention of a product (muscle-enhancing shirt) as a shortcut, but the former editor of Men's Fitness magazine criticizes it, advocating for actual exercise. The passage concludes by suggesting that while exercise is good, character is more important than appearance. The author then mentions their own intention to go to the gym for health reasons, not just to get a six-pack, to improve confidence. The question is asking what the passage mainly talks about, with options A to D. Let's break down the options: A) You shouldn't judge a book by its cover. B) More and more people become obese in England. C) Role models put great pressure on common people. D) Men become more and more conscious of their body image. The passage does mention that character is more important than appearance towards the end, which relates to option A. But the main focus seems to be on the survey about men's body image concerns and the societal pressures leading to that. Option C talks about role models putting pressure, which is mentioned as a reason for the body image issues. Option D says men are becoming more conscious, which is the central point of the survey and the discussion. Option B about obesity is touched upon but not the main topic. The survey is about body image dissatisfaction, not specifically obesity rates. The muscle-enhancing shirt and the editor's comment are part of the discussion on how men are trying to address their body image issues, which supports the idea that men are more conscious of their body image. So the main topic is D. Option C is
